 {"id":"bd-2ad","title":"Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)","description":"# Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)

## GOAL
Implement robust mount lifecycle management with clean startup, shutdown, and signal handling.

## BACKGROUND
Production filesystems must handle:
- Clean mount with validation
- Graceful unmount with flush
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Crash recovery on restart

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Phase
```rust
pub async fn mount(config: MountConfig, cx: &Cx) -> Result<MountHandle> {
    // 1. Validate image (superblock, journal state)
    // 2. Replay journal if needed (ext4)
    // 3. Initialize MVCC store
    // 4. Initialize ARC cache
    // 5. Initialize repair manager
    // 6. Start background tasks (scrub, GC)
    // 7. Mount FUSE session
    // 8. Return handle for lifecycle control
}
```

### 2. Unmount Phase
```rust
impl MountHandle {
    pub async fn unmount(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Stop accepting new requests
        // 2. Wait for in-flight requests (with timeout)
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries
        // 4. Commit pending transactions
        // 5. Refresh repair symbols for dirty groups
        // 6. Close FUSE session
        // 7. Sync and close device
    }
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: Trigger graceful unmount
- SIGUSR1: Dump debug state to log
- Timeout for unmount (default 30s, then force)

### 4. Drop Safety
- MountHandle::drop must trigger unmount
- Panic in unmount must not corrupt filesystem
- Leaked handle must eventually timeout

## TESTS
1. Unit: Mount/unmount roundtrip
2. Integration: SIGTERM during I/O results in clean state
3. Integration: Double-mount same image fails cleanly
4. Stress: Mount/unmount loop 100x without leaks

## LOGGING
- Mount: log config, image path, mount point
- Unmount: log flush stats, pending ops
- Signal: log signal received, action taken

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount ... && ffs umount ...` leaves clean state
2. `kill -TERM $(pidof ffs)` results in clean unmount
3. Remount after crash shows no corruption
4. No resource leaks (file descriptors, memory)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:34.171965858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310382109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310314994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","lifecycle","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:06.032850244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.263209229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2ad","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This bead covers the 'FUSE mount runtime' row (currently ❌). When complete, flip to ✅ and update ParityReport::current() counts.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2c1y","title":"MVCC durability: persist WAL before exposing commit","description":"Context: current PersistentMvccStore commit flow applies to in-memory store before WAL append, creating crash window where acknowledged commit is lost on replay.\n\nScope:\n- Reorder commit pipeline so WAL append/sync succeeds before in-memory visibility of commit.\n- Ensure WAL append failure returns error and does not expose transaction versions.\n- Add regression tests for WAL failure path and replay durability.\n\nAcceptance:\n- commit() does not mutate visible store state when WAL append fails.\n- successful commit remains replayable after restart.\n- targeted tests pass via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:07:05.755076103Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539117230Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539092574Z","close_reason":"Completed: WAL failure now rolls back in-memory commit state; mvcc durability tests and workspace gates pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","durability","mvcc","wal"]}
@@ -500,7 +500,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-7tn4","title":"PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)","description":"# PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)

## GOAL
Implement Proofs of Retrievability (PoR) for cryptographic verification that stored data is intact and retrievable, without reading the entire dataset.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 11.11: PoR (Juels-Kaliski 2007, Shacham-Waters 2008) allows a verifier to challenge a storage system with random queries. The storage system responds with proofs that specific blocks exist and are correct. If the proofs verify, the entire dataset is retrievable with high probability.

## DESIGN
- Challenge: verifier sends random block indices + random coefficients
- Response: storage computes linear combination of challenged blocks weighted by coefficients
- Verification: verifier checks response against precomputed authenticators
- Authenticators: computed once at file creation, stored alongside data

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: useful for cloud/remote storage verification
- Complementary to RaptorQ: PoR proves data exists, RaptorQ repairs it if it doesn't
- Useful for auditing: periodic PoR challenges prove filesystem integrity without full scan

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PoR authenticator generation at file creation
- [ ] Challenge-response protocol implemented
- [ ] Verification: false positive rate < 2^-128
- [ ] Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file","acceptance_criteria":"PoR authenticator generation at file creation time. Challenge-response protocol implemented with configurable challenge count. Verification: false positive rate below 2^-128. Authenticators stored alongside data in reserved metadata area. Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file. Benchmark: authenticator generation time per MB. Unit tests: generate authenticators, challenge, verify for small files. Unit tests: corrupted block causes verification failure. Unit tests: uncorrupted file passes all challenges. Property test: random challenge sets always verify on intact data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:32.840799452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585683263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585575381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","durability","por"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.828805088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7xja","title":"Reject zero stripe_len in btrfs chunk parsing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:23:39.608244456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T03:41:37.759251284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T03:41:37.758997233Z","close_reason":"Completed in 2b9bf87","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7xzs7","title":"add kernel-reference and operator-path coverage for ext4 POSIX ACL xattrs","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-xattr` parses and lists `system.posix_acl_access` / `system.posix_acl_default`, and README claims `system.*` coverage for POSIX ACLs, but the harness coverage is still concentrated on `user.*` plus `security.selinux`. There is no kernel-reference or mounted-path proof for ACL namespace behavior.

Scope:
- Extend `crates/ffs-harness/tests/kernel_reference.rs` to create ACL xattrs through `debugfs` and compare list/get behavior against FrankenFS.
- Add operator-path coverage for ACL xattrs where the environment has the needed privilege, with deterministic soft-skip behavior when `CAP_SYS_ADMIN` is unavailable.
- Cover both access and default ACL names, plus at least one malformed or missing-value edge case.
- Update parity/docs wording so ACL support claims match tested surface exactly.

Acceptance:
- ACL namespace behavior is proven against kernel tooling, not only crate-local parsing tests.
- Mounted-path coverage or explicit privileged soft-skip evidence exists for the operator-visible path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:25.632276975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:44:23.140332702Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:44:23.138068285Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acl","ext4","fuse","testing","xattr"]}

 {"id":"bd-spik","title":"Implement 6 parity features: btrfs decompression, ext4 inline/indirect/bigalloc/ZERO_RANGE, btrfs subvol","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:08.050698719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:23.369345832Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:57:23.368707223Z","close_reason":"Full parity session: implemented 6 new filesystem features to close gaps between FrankenFS and real ext4/btrfs images:

1. btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB + ZSTD) — compressed extents now readable
2. ext4 inline data read — EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL files now readable from inode body + xattr
3. ext4 indirect block addressing — pre-extent inodes with direct/indirect/double/triple pointers
4. ext4 bigalloc infrastructure — cluster_ratio field in FsGeometry
5. ext4 FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE — new fallocate mode for zeroing allocated ranges
6. btrfs subvolume mount — configurable subvol_objectid in BtrfsContext

Also fixed during review: compressed extent size overflow, decompressed length validation, inline data xattr parsing (use parse_ibody_xattrs instead of naive offset skip).

Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md: 80/80 tracked features (was 75/75).
473 ffs-core tests pass. All leaf crates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-sqc5g","title":"add mounted-path ext4 hard-link refusal coverage for occupied destination EEXIST and non-directory parent ENOTDIR","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success plus some refusal paths, but it does not freeze the operator-visible errors for linking onto an occupied destination or into a non-directory parent even though ffs-core already enforces EEXIST and ENOTDIR. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that assert hard_link over an existing destination returns EEXIST and hard_link into a regular-file parent returns ENOTDIR, with unchanged nlink and directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 hard-link refusal behavior for occupied-destination and non-directory-parent cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:09:23.154185107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:11:49.540633974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sqtrq","title":"stress fuse inode lock ordering under concurrent requests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:34:13.423718762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.963392029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T01:22:03.960982426Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:52:18.357666781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:52:18.357666781Z","close_reason":"Verified already implemented on current main: focused ffs-core and ffs-cli mount-selection tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
-{"id":"bd-sv2c3","title":"ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags to OpenOptions","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-cli` advertises `--subvol`/`--snapshot` on `mount`, but `validate_btrfs_mount_selection()` hard-rejects both flags even though the underlying codebase already has internal btrfs subvolume context machinery. This bead owns the operator-facing CLI plumbing.

Scope:
- Remove the unconditional rejection path in `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- Thread the requested selection into `OpenOptions` and preserve all existing ext4 behavior.
- Emit structured startup/rejection logs that show requested selector, resolved selection type, and failure class.
- Replace the current `mount_rejects_subvol_flag` / `mount_rejects_snapshot_flag` tests with positive routing tests and deterministic failure diagnostics for nonexistent selectors.

Acceptance:
- `ffs mount ... --subvol <name>` and `ffs mount ... --snapshot <name>` reach `OpenFs::open_with_options` instead of bailing in argument validation.
- Missing selectors fail with clear, stable user-facing errors.
- CLI tests cover default-root, explicit subvolume, explicit snapshot, and nonexistent selection cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","mount"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv2c3","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-sv1b2","title":"btrfs core: resolve named subvolume and snapshot mount selections","description":"Survey finding: `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` both claim btrfs subvolume mount support, but `OpenFs::from_device` still hardcodes `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` and the operator-facing CLI rejects `--subvol/--snapshot`. This bead owns the core selection logic in `ffs-core`.

Scope:
- Extend `OpenOptions` with an explicit btrfs mount selection contract (default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and optionally numeric objectid if that simplifies diagnostics).
- Walk the root tree and resolve the requested selection to the mounted `subvol_objectid` plus `subvol_root_dirid`.
- Return deterministic `FfsError` variants for missing selections, ambiguous names, and selection/type mismatches.
- Keep default-root behavior unchanged when no selection is requested.

Acceptance:
- `OpenFs::open_with_options` can mount a non-default subvolume/snapshot without post-open mutation hacks.
- Synthetic and/or kernel-reference tests prove correct resolution for default root, named subvolume, named snapshot, and missing target cases.
- Structured diagnostics include the requested selector and resolved objectid/root dirid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:40:14.299688630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","mount","parity"]}
+{"id":"bd-sv2c3","title":"ffs-cli: wire btrfs --subvol and --snapshot mount flags to OpenOptions","description":"Survey finding: `ffs-cli` advertises `--subvol`/`--snapshot` on `mount`, but `validate_btrfs_mount_selection()` hard-rejects both flags even though the underlying codebase already has internal btrfs subvolume context machinery. This bead owns the operator-facing CLI plumbing.

Scope:
- Remove the unconditional rejection path in `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`.
- Thread the requested selection into `OpenOptions` and preserve all existing ext4 behavior.
- Emit structured startup/rejection logs that show requested selector, resolved selection type, and failure class.
- Replace the current `mount_rejects_subvol_flag` / `mount_rejects_snapshot_flag` tests with positive routing tests and deterministic failure diagnostics for nonexistent selectors.

Acceptance:
- `ffs mount ... --subvol <name>` and `ffs mount ... --snapshot <name>` reach `OpenFs::open_with_options` instead of bailing in argument validation.
- Missing selectors fail with clear, stable user-facing errors.

 {"id":"bd-sv3d4","title":"add operator-facing coverage and parity evidence for btrfs subvolume/snapshot mount","description":"Survey finding: current conformance only proves the default btrfs root-alias path, while docs/parity wording imply user-selectable subvolume mount support. This bead closes that evidence gap after the core and CLI work land.

Scope:
- Add harness and/or FUSE E2E coverage that mounts a non-default btrfs subvolume and snapshot through the real CLI/operator path.
- Assert mounted-root `getattr`/`lookup`/`readdir`/`read` behavior is scoped to the selected tree, not the default root.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and README wording so the operator-facing support claim matches the tested surface exactly.
- Ensure test output/logs emit machine-parseable scenario identifiers for success and failure cases.

Acceptance:
- There is deterministic coverage for mounting a named subvolume and a named snapshot via the public mount path.
- README and `FEATURE_PARITY.md` no longer overclaim relative to the tested operator surface.
- Coverage artifacts make it clear whether support is internal-only or operator-visible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","docs","e2e","mount","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv1b2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sv3d4","depends_on_id":"bd-sv2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Acceptance: mounted-path fallocate coverage distinguishes invalid punch-hole mode from unsupported extra-bit rejection on both filesystems.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:11:26.592121204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:11:26.592121204Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-szi0z","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for regular file writes","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e exercises writable file creation and overwrite paths, and ffs-core already returns ReadOnly/EROFS for write on a read-only mount, but the mounted-path public write refusal contract is not frozen.
